pages.text: The seasonal year in Novus is $(bold)96 days$() — four seasons of three sub-seasons each, eight days per sub-season. New worlds begin in $(italic)Early Spring$().
pages.title: The Calendar
The Wheel of the Year
pages.text: Twelve sub-seasons, each with its own color. The wheel turns clockwise from Early Spring at the top.

$(bold)The four seasons, in brief:$()$(br2)$(italic)Spring$() — generous to a beginning farmer. All overworld crops grow, Dynamic Trees grow fastest, rain is common.$(br2)$(italic)Summer$() — long days, frequent thunder. All fruit-bearing crops thrive. Snow and ice MELT in the open.

$(italic)Autumn$() — the gathering season. Most crops still bear; slow down by Late Autumn. Time to lay in stews for winter.$(br2)$(italic)Winter$() — real winter. Temperature drops by −0.8 everywhere; snow falls in places that wouldn't normally see it. NO thunderstorms. Open-air crops crawl at ~10% speed.

$(bold)Two ways to keep crops growing in winter:$()$(br2)$(li)$(bold)Underground$() — any plot at or below Y=48 is treated as in-season year-round$(li)$(bold)Under glass$() — any block tagged as glass (vanilla, stained, framed, modded) anywhere in the 16 blocks directly above a crop tells the season system that crop is in-season. A glass-roofed greenhouse works perfectly.
pages.title: Winter Farming

pages.text: $(bold)Seasons pause when the game closes.$() Time and the year only advance while the game is actively running. Closing to title or quitting freezes everything where it is — including season progression. This is intentional.
pages.title: Seasons Pause
